_meta.source: The Library Canonical Philosophy — Derivation Chain
_meta.description: The 7-step formal derivation from axiom through six overtones. Each step has a proof from a specific domain of mathematics or formal logic. These are not philosophical arguments — they are formal necessities.
_meta.usage: Enables 'why' answers in synthesis. When the user asks WHY a chart element behaves as it does, the derivation chain provides the structural reasoning.

axiom.nature: Not 'I think therefore I am' (imports assumptions about the thinker). The irreducible datum: something is aware. Science cannot disprove it because science requires it as a precondition.
axiom.structure: Contains two terms: awareness (experiential pole) and is (ontological pole). These two terms do not meet and do not need to. They need to be held. 'Awareness is' IS the first polarity — the irreducible charge between experiencing and existing.
axiom.harmonicBasis: The rapid reflection between these two poles — awareness registering its own is-ness, existence registering its own awareness — is itself a vibration. Each cycle of reflection raises the frequency. This is the harmonic series at its most primitive: the fundamental tone vibrating against its own existence, generating overtones.

derivationChain.proofSketch: Shannon (1948): information content requires at minimum two distinguishable states. H=0 with one state — no information, no experience possible. Bateson (1972): information is 'a difference that makes a difference.' Any system capable of experience requires at minimum two mutually distinguishable states = the formal definition of polarity.
derivationChain.intervalProof: 2:1 = 'two ones.' Same pitch class, double frequency. Identical in nature, different in magnitude. This IS polarity by mathematical definition.
derivationChain.whyItMatters: Every defined/undefined center pair in a chart is a polarity. Understanding this structurally means neither state is 'better' — they are two poles of a charge relationship.

derivationChain.proofSketch: Russell (1908): any system containing P and ¬P generates self-referential paradoxes unless a metalevel is introduced. Any polaric system (A and ¬A) creates recursive self-reference producing paradox without a Third term. Nash (1950): any two-player system requires an equilibrium condition — a third term — for stability.
derivationChain.status: Formally proven. Russell's type theory (1908); Nash (1950) — convergent multi-domain proof.
derivationChain.intervalProof: Numerator 3 = Trinity. The Pythagorean comma proves Trinity exceeds Polarity: (3/2)^12 ≠ 2^7 — the three-body system cannot close on the two-body container.
derivationChain.whyItMatters: Type + Strategy + Authority is a trinity. Explaining any two without the third produces paradox. The emergent 'Third' is the person's unique behavioral quality.

derivationChain.proofSketch: Three non-collinear points define a plane (2D interior, no depth). In 3D Euclidean space, four non-coplanar points are the minimum required to enclose a volume. The tetrahedron (4 vertices, 6 edges, 4 faces) is the first structure with genuine interior: containment.
derivationChain.status: Formally proven. Euclidean geometry (tetrahedron as minimum 3D container).
derivationChain.intervalProof: Numerator 4 = Quaternity. The Fourth is the octave complement of the Fifth (4/3 × 3/2 = 2/1) — the containing interval.
derivationChain.whyItMatters: Four motors, four awareness centers, four types — these quaternary structures create the chart's containment. They hold the person's energy configuration inside a genuine interior.

derivationChain.proofSketch: Quaternity's centroid is K₁,₄ (star graph: hub + 4 leaves, acyclic). Phi requires graph cycles (Fibonacci self-reference). K₁,₄ is acyclic — Phi impossible. C₅ (pentagon: 5 vertices in closed ring) has cycles — Phi necessary. The categorical topological transition K₁,₄ → C₅ makes Phi possible for the first time.
derivationChain.status: Formally proven. Graph topology + Fibonacci limit theorem.
derivationChain.whyItMatters: Channel completion is this exact topological shift: from star (hanging gates reaching outward) to ring (completed channel cycling energy). The shift is categorical, not gradual.

derivationChain.proofSketch: In 2D Euclidean space, maximum circles of equal size touching a central circle = exactly 6 (6 × 60° = 360°). Hales (1998, verified 2017): hexagonal arrangement is the uniquely optimal packing. Pentadic entities in optimal community → hexagonal packing is the necessary consequence.
derivationChain.status: Formally proven. Apollonius (kissing number in 2D); Hales 1998/2017.
derivationChain.whyItMatters: Circuit groups organize individuals into maximally efficient communities. When a circuit closes, it achieves hexagonal closure — a community frequency that open circuits cannot generate.

derivationChain.proofSketch: The 7th harmonic (7:4) is the first overtone unrepresentable in the diatonic system. Prime 7 appears nowhere in diatonic ratios (only 2, 3, 5). The harmonic series overflows the diatonic container at 7. The system must either return to fundamental at higher octave (Ti → Do) or develop a new containing system (diatonic → chromatic).
derivationChain.status: Formally proven. Harmonic series mathematics (7th prime as first non-diatonic overflow).
derivationChain.intervalProof: 15 = 3×5 (Trinity × Pentad). 8 = 2³ (Polarity-cubed). Product of most generative principles over cube of foundational differentiation. Plus: 15:8 × 16:15 = 2:1 — one minimum half-step from octave.
derivationChain.whyItMatters: The full chart as a unified system generates a truth (the person's total pattern) that no subsystem can contain. This overflow IS self-awareness. The synthesis is the Heptad moment — Ti resolving to Do.

intervalSummary.method: All six use ratio-numerator correspondence (Method A). The Major Seventh's proof is algebraically richer — 15=3×5, 8=2³ — but it is still arithmetic encoding, not qualitative analogy.

assignments.proof: 2:1 = two ones = polarity by mathematical definition
Numerator 4 = Quaternity. Octave complement of Fifth

chromaticCompletion.pianoAnalogy: 5 black keys (pentatonic) + 7 white keys (diatonic) = 12 keys (chromatic). The pentatonic and diatonic are each other's chromatic complement.
chromaticCompletion.librarySelection: The Library's 7 levels = diatonic: minimum selection including all five primary interval relationships AND a leading tone for directed return.
chromaticCompletion.primeSelfSelection: Prime Self's 5 Forges = pentatonic: the globally most universal scale, appearing independently across all cultures. The five patterns arising naturally from the logic of living form.
chromaticCompletion.knowledgesSelection: Prime Self's 6 Knowledges = whole-tone (hexatonic): six equal divisions of the octave, maximum symmetry, minimum tension.
chromaticCompletion.selfAs12th: Self (6th Forge / 7th Knowledge) = the 12th position, the return Do completing the chromatic scale.
